part.p: Establish and manage an updated inventory of third-party components used in development, often referred to as a “bill of materials,” as well as components slated for future use. This inventory is to include any risks that each third-party component could pose. Evaluate the list at least monthly to identify any changes or updates to these components, and validate that the component is still supported.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: An updated inventory of third-party components used in development is established and maintained, often referred to as a “bill of materials,” as well as components slated for future use.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The inventory includes any risks that each third-party component could pose.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The list is evaluated at least monthly to identify any changes or updates to these components, and validate that the component is still supported.

control.title: Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components

part.name: statement
part.p: Use up-to-date and trusted third-party software components. When possible, choose established and proven frameworks and libraries that provide adequate security. Acquire these components from trusted sources or evaluate the software for vulnerabilities before use.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Up-to-date and trusted third-party software components are used.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Established and proven frameworks and libraries are chosen that provide adequate security, when possible.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Components are acquired from trusted sources or software is evaluated for vulnerabilities before use.

control.title: Establish and Maintain a Severity Rating System and Process for Application Vulnerabilities

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain a severity rating system and process for application vulnerabilities that facilitates prioritizing the order in which discovered vulnerabilities are fixed. This process includes setting a minimum level of security acceptability for releasing code or applications. Severity ratings bring a systematic way of triaging vulnerabilities that improves risk management and helps ensure the most severe bugs are fixed first. Review and update the system and process annually.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A severity rating system and process is established and maintained for application vulnerabilities.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The severity rating system facilitates prioritizing the order in which discovered vulnerabilities are fixed.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process includes setting a minimum level of security acceptability for releasing code or applications.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The system and process are reviewed and updated annually.

control.title: Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure

part.name: statement
part.p: Use standard, industry-recommended hardening configuration templates for application infrastructure components. This includes underlying servers, databases, and web servers, and applies to cloud containers, Platform as a Service (PaaS) components, and SaaS components. Do not allow in-house developed software to weaken configuration hardening.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Standard, industry-recommended hardening configuration templates are used for application infrastructure components.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The configuration templates apply to underlying servers, databases, and web servers, and applies to cloud containers, Platform as a Service (PaaS) components, and SaaS components.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: In-house developed software does not weaken configuration hardening.

part.name: statement
part.p: Maintain separate environments for production and non-production systems.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Separate environments are maintained for production and non-production systems.

control.title: Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding

part.name: statement
part.p: Ensure that all software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities. Training can include general security principles and application security standard practices. Conduct training at least annually and design in a way to promote security within the development team, and build a culture of security among the developers.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: All software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Training is designed in a way to promote security within the development team, and build a culture of security among the developers.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Training is conducted at least annually.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Training can include general security principles and application security standard practices.

control.title: Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures

part.name: statement
part.p: Apply secure design principles in application architectures. Secure design principles include the concept of least privilege and enforcing mediation to validate every operation that the user makes, promoting the concept of "never trust user input." Examples include ensuring that explicit error checking is performed and documented for all input, including for size, data type, and acceptable ranges or formats. Secure design also means minimizing the application infrastructure attack surface, such as turning off unprotected ports and services, removing unnecessary programs and files, and renaming or removing default accounts.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Secure design principles are applied in application architectures.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Secure design principles include the concept of least privilege and enforcing mediation to validate every operation that the user makes, promoting the concept of "never trust user input."

Secure design also includes minimizing the application infrastructure attack surface, such as turning off unprotected ports and services, removing unnecessary programs and files, and renaming or removing default accounts.

part.name: example
part.p: Examples include ensuring that explicit error checking is performed and documented for all input, including for size, data type, and acceptable ranges or formats.

control.title: Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components

part.name: statement
part.p: Leverage vetted modules or services for application security components, such as identity management, encryption, auditing, and logging. Using platform features in critical security functions will reduce developers' workload and minimize the likelihood of design or implementation errors. Modern operating systems provide effective mechanisms for identification, authentication, and authorization and make those mechanisms available to applications. Use only standardized, currently accepted, and extensively reviewed encryption algorithms. Operating systems also provide mechanisms to create and maintain secure audit logs.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Vetted modules or services are used for application security components, such as identity management, encryption, auditing, and logging.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Only standardized, currently accepted, and extensively reviewed encryption algorithms are used.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Using platform features in critical security functions will reduce developers’ workload and minimize the likelihood of design or implementation errors. Modern operating systems provide effective mechanisms for identification, authentication, and authorization and make those mechanisms available to applications.

part.name: statement
part.p: Apply static and dynamic analysis tools within the application life cycle to verify that secure coding practices are being followed.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Static and dynamic analysis tools are applied within the application life cycle to verify that secure coding practices are being followed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Conduct application penetration testing. For critical applications, authenticated penetration testing is better suited to finding business logic vulnerabilities than code scanning and automated security testing. Penetration testing relies on the skill of the tester to manually manipulate an application as an authenticated and unauthenticated user.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Application penetration testing is conducted.

part.name: guidance
part.p: For critical applications, authenticated penetration testing is better suited to finding business logic vulnerabilities than code scanning and automated security testing.
Penetration testing relies on the skill of the tester to manually manipulate an application as an authenticated and unauthenticated user.

part.name: statement
part.p: Conduct threat modeling. Threat modeling is the process of identifying and addressing application security design flaws within a design, before code is created. It is conducted through specially trained individuals who evaluate the application design and gauge security risks for each entry point and access level. The goal is to map out the application, architecture, and infrastructure in a structured way to understand its weaknesses.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Threat modeling is conducted.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Threat modeling is the process of identifying and addressing application security design flaws within a design, before code is created. It is conducted through specially trained individuals who evaluate the application design and gauge security risks for each entry point and access level. The goal is to map out the application, architecture, and infrastructure in a structured way to understand its weaknesses.

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability (e.g., policies, plans, procedures, defined roles, training, and communications) to prepare, detect, and quickly respond to an attack.

control.title: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling

part.name: statement
part.p: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process. Management personnel are responsible for the coordination and documentation of incident response and recovery efforts and can consist of employees internal to the enterprise, service providers, or a hybrid approach. If using a service provider, designate at least one person internal to the enterprise to oversee any third-party work. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: One key person, and at least one backup, are designated who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Management personnel are responsible for the coordination and documentation of incident response and recovery efforts and consist of employees internal to the enterprise, service providers, or a hybrid approach.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: If using a service provider, at least one person internal to the enterprise is designated to oversee any third-party work.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A list of personnel is reviewed annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

control.title: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, service providers, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Contact information is established and maintained for parties that need to be informed of security incidents.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Contacts are verified annually to ensure that information is up to date.

part.name: example
part.p: Contacts may include internal staff, service providers, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders.

control.title: Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain an documented enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents. The process includes reporting timeframe, personnel to report to, mechanism for reporting, and the minimum information to be reported. Ensure the process is publicly available to all of the workforce. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A documented enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents is established and maintained.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process includes reporting timeframe, personnel to report to, mechanism for reporting, and the minimum information to be reported.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process is publicly available to all of the workforce.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process is reviewed annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

control.title: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain a documented incident response process that addresses roles and responsibilities, compliance requirements, and a communication plan. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A documented incident response process is established and maintained.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process addresses roles and responsibilities, compliance requirements, and a communication plan.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The process is reviewed annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: statement
part.p: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response, including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, public relations, human resources, incident responders, analysts, and relevant third parties. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Key roles and responsibilities are assigned for incident response, including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, public relations, human resources, incident responders, analysts, and relevant third parties.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The roles and responsibilities are reviewed annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

control.title: Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response

part.name: statement
part.p: Determine which primary and secondary mechanisms will be used to communicate and report during a security incident. Mechanisms can include phone calls, emails, secure chat, or notification letters. Keep in mind that certain mechanisms, such as emails, can be affected during a security incident. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Primary and secondary mechanisms used to communicate and report during a security incident are determined.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Mechanisms are reviewed annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: example
part.p: Mechanisms can include phone calls, emails, secure chat, or notification letters.
Keep in mind that certain mechanisms, such as emails, can be affected during a security incident.

part.name: statement
part.p: Plan and conduct routine incident response exercises and scenarios for key personnel involved in the incident response process to prepare for responding to real-world incidents. Exercises need to test communication channels, decision making, and workflows. Conduct testing on an annual basis, at a minimum.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Routine incident response exercises and scenarios are planned and conducted for key personnel involved in the incident response process to prepare for responding to real-world incidents.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: The exercises test communication channels, decision-making, and workflows.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Testing is conducted on an annual basis, at a minimum.

part.name: statement
part.p: Conduct post-incident reviews. Post-incident reviews help prevent incident recurrence through identifying lessons learned and follow-up action.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Post-incident reviews are conducted.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Post-incident reviews help prevent incident recurrence through identifying lessons learned and follow-up action.

control.title: Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain security incident thresholds, including, at a minimum, differentiating between an incident and an event. Examples can include: abnormal activity, security vulnerability, security weakness, data breach, privacy incident, etc. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Security incident thresholds are established and maintained, including, at a minimum, differentiating between an incident and an event.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Review security incident thresholds annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

part.name: example
part.p: Examples can include: abnormal activity, security vulnerability, security weakness, data breach, privacy incident, etc.

part.name: statement
part.p: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls (people, processes, and technology), and simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

control.title: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program

part.name: statement
part.p: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, industry, and maturity of the enterprise. Penetration testing program characteristics include scope, such as network, web application, Application Programming Interface (API), hosted services, and physical premise controls; frequency; limitations, such as acceptable hours, and excluded attack types; point of contact information; remediation, such as how findings will be routed internally; and retrospective requirements.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A penetration testing program is established and maintained appropriate to the size, complexity, industry, and maturity of the enterprise.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Penetration testing program characteristics include scope, such as network, web application, Application Programming Interface (API), hosted services, and physical premise controls; frequency; limitations, such as acceptable hours, and excluded attack types; point of contact information; remediation, such as how findings will be routed internally; and retrospective requirements.

part.name: statement
part.p: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. External penetration testing must include enterprise and environmental reconnaissance to detect exploitable information. Penetration testing requires specialized skills and experience and must be conducted through a qualified party. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Periodic external penetration tests are performed based on program requirements.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Periodic external penetration tests are performed no less than annually.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: External penetration testing includes enterprise and environmental reconnaissance to detect exploitable information.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Penetration testing is conducted through a qualified party who has specialized skills and experience.

part.name: guidance
part.p: The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

part.name: statement
part.p: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise's documented vulnerability remediation process. This should include determining a timeline and level of effort based on the impact and prioritization of each identified finding.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Penetration test findings are remediated based on the enterprise's documented vulnerability remediation process.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: A timeline and level of effort are determined based on the impact and prioritization of each identified finding.

part.name: statement
part.p: Validate security measures after each penetration test. If deemed necessary, modify rulesets and capabilities to detect the techniques used during testing.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Security measures are validated after each penetration test.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: If deemed necessary, rulesets and capabilities are modified to detect the techniques used during testing.

part.name: statement
part.p: Perform periodic internal penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Periodic internal penetration tests are performed based on program requirements.

part.name: assessment-objective
part.p: Periodic internal penetration tests are performed no less than annually.

part.name: guidance
part.p: The testing may be clear box or opaque box.
